module_mood_contract: operational clarity — organiser feels they own the customer-side workspace, folder test, grouping, and readout path; not a fourth participant module, but the runbook included because the customer gets one workbook

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All teaching beats score 8/10. Setup/sequencing beats (Start Here, Close) are mood-exempt by rule. Operational clarity holds: each phase delivers a tested workspace or a clear synthesis path. No beat flattens into participant-module register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M4 must not feel tidy.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is the organiser runbook appended to the customer workbook. Its mood is operational clarity (owning the room mechanics), not participant completion. The module does not resolve M3's capable-next-step mood early; it is a separate audience path that exists alongside, not after, the participant arc. No tidy graduation ceremony shape detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module's teaching moment per strategy doc: customer-side operation is part of the system; the trainer shows the Bosser system, organisers prove and run the customer workspace. This lands via the exercise chain — prepare first (Phase 1-5 of exercise), synthesize second. The sequencing is structurally enforced by exercise ordering, not by body prose, so a fast-reading organiser cannot skip the prepare step and land at synthesis without the workspace existing. Teaching moment is robust.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honours its strategy-doc Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc Big Idea: 'The same workbook carries the organiser runbook, but organiser work stays separate from the participant path.' File's ## Big Idea matches verbatim: 'The same workbook carries the organiser runbook, but organiser work stays separate from the participant path.' The Start Here section reinforces the separation ('The live-room participant path does not need this section'). Fidelity confirmed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off correctly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is the final module in the training. No M5 exists. The Close sentence ('the workbook is ready for delivery') correctly terminates the training arc without projecting a next module that doesn't exist. Strategy doc confirms M4 is the organiser runbook with no downstream module. N/A for inter-module handoff; terminal arc position is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module body contains no authored conversation cues, pause prompts, group-discussion directives, or room-share sentences. All body prose names the work (what the organiser sets up, what the synthesis exercise produces). No banned phrases detected ('share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'sitting next to you').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module-level body has no forcing function prose; it routes to exercises where forcing functions live inside fenced prompts. The module wrapper correctly delegates to included exercises rather than narrating the mechanics in body prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood not punitive — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 mood is operational clarity, not unease. Rule is N/A for this module position.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: What This Section Builds bullets: Prepare / Test / Run / Keep — all positive discipline verbs. No failure-mode framing in the LO bullets. 'Keep customer material inside the customer's SharePoint and Cowork environment' names a positive discipline (boundary-keeping), not a failure-avoidance frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a module wrapper, not a standalone exercise. Leap tests belong in the included exercise maintainer blocks (organisers-prepare-claude-basics.md, organisers-synthesize-rollout.md), not in the module wrapper. N/A at module level.
Module wrapper has no phase prompts and no forcing functions of its own. Failure-mode coverage belongs in the included exercises' maintainer blocks. N/A at module wrapper level.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module has no ## Key Concepts section. It is an organiser runbook section (M4), not a participant module with emergent Key Concepts. The What This Section Builds section carries the LOs; no Key Concepts section is expected or present. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement — meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module contains no lectures. Two exercise inclusions only. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register, not syllabus.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The section uses 'What This Section Builds' (not 'What You'll Learn') — organiser-appropriate heading variant. Bullets: Prepare / Test / Run / Keep — all verb-led, concrete, capability register. No Bloom-tag parentheticals, no mechanism tails, no apparatus failure modes detected. The fourth bullet ('Keep customer material inside the customer's SharePoint and Cowork environment') is a constraint/boundary statement rather than a capability verb — marginally weak, but acceptable for a privacy-boundary LO in an organiser section.
